concepts.title: Measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode)
concepts.hook: Understand data location; critical for detecting when to use robust statistics.

concepts.title: Measures of spread (variance, std-dev, IQR)
concepts.hook: Quantify data dispersion; essential for identifying outliers.

concepts.title: Skewness and kurtosis
concepts.hook: Detect non-normal distributions; shapes downstream model assumptions.

concepts.title: Covariance and correlation
concepts.hook: Measure linear relationships; foundation for feature engineering.

concepts.title: Bayes' theorem and conditional probability
concepts.hook: Core to hypothesis testing and classification interpretability.

concepts.title: Frequentist vs Bayesian interpretation
concepts.hook: Defines how you construct confidence intervals and credible intervals differently.

concepts.title: Law of large numbers and Central Limit Theorem
concepts.hook: Justifies why sample means are normal; critical for hypothesis tests.

concepts.title: Prior, likelihood, posterior (Bayesian framework)
concepts.hook: Foundation for Bayesian stats, A/B test interpretation, and generative models.

concepts.title: Normal distribution and its properties
concepts.hook: Most common assumption in statistics; basis for linear regression inference.

concepts.title: Binomial and Poisson distributions
concepts.hook: Model discrete counts; used in conversion modeling.

concepts.title: Beta distribution
concepts.hook: Prior for proportions; underpins empirical Bayes and online learning.

concepts.title: Exponential and Weibull distributions
concepts.hook: Model time-to-event; used in survival analysis and failure rate modeling.

concepts.title: Student's t-distribution
concepts.hook: Accounts for small samples; basis for t-tests and regression inference.

concepts.title: Sampling bias and representative samples
concepts.hook: Selection bias contaminates inference; critical to study design.

concepts.title: Power analysis and sample size calculation
concepts.hook: Determines how large a sample you need for adequate statistical power.

concepts.title: Stratified sampling and blocking
concepts.hook: Reduces variance in RCTs; improves experiment efficiency.

concepts.title: Observational vs experimental data
concepts.hook: Only RCTs enable causal inference without strong assumptions.
